title: Gooey Festive British Beef Shin Stir-fry
description: This high-protein-friendly dish features gooey beef shin stir-fried with fermented sauces for a festive twist in the UK culinary tradition.

ingredients.name: 1 lb Beef Shin (sourced from grass-fed animals)
ingredients.notes: Ensure the meat is at safe handling temperatures

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup Fermented Black Beans
ingredients.notes: Use a reputable brand to ensure safety and quality

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons Soy Sauce (low sodium or gluten-free)
1/4 cup Fermented Black Garlic (if available, optional)

instructions: Preheat your wok or large frying pan over high heat and add oil.
Add minced garlic to the hot pan and stir for about a minute until fragrant.
Place the beef in the pan and sear on both sides, cooking until browned. Remove from the pan and set aside.
In the same pan, add fermented black beans, Hoisin sauce, oyster sauce, and if using, fermented black garlic, and stir to combine with the oil and spices.

Return the beef shin to the pan, adding it back to the mixture evenly distributed. If needed, pour a little more vegetable oil over the top of the ingredients in the pan for added moisture (be cautious as the beef is already partially cooked).
Cover the pan and cook until the beef is fully heated through and sauce thickened slightly.
Add finely chopped scallions to the pan and stir for about 30 seconds, allowing them to wilt but not overcook to maintain their bright green color.
Remove from heat and serve hot with your choice of side dishes such as steamed vegetables or rice.
